source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.460 (scan idx 459), TABVLA XX
family: compendium (18 numbered sub-figures: rebus, music, 3D cube, sphere, letter-grids; plus a multilingual logogriph key)
title_diplomatic: TABVLA XX — Figura prima ... Figura decima octava; CVBVS METAMETRICVS; LOGOGRIPHORVM EXPOSITIO
note: The richest single plate. Contains the CUBVS METAMETRICVS (a 3D wireframe cube lettered A-E for tri-axial spatial reading) and a spherical metametric labyrinth (Figura 18: a globe of lettered great-circles with central value-key A-vu / B-uu / C / D / E / F / G-vu / H). Figures I-XIV are rebuses decoded by the LOGOGRIPHORVM EXPOSITIO key, which is MULTILINGUAL (Latin, Spanish, French).

logogriph_key.III: Consuelate Corazon, si has passion y espira, que el Mundo rueda
logogriph_key.IV: Lauro y corona a las letras
logogriph_key.V: De mi amor o de mi vida sera fin
logogriph_key.VI: Et libri et gladii errores carpebimus orbis
logogriph_key.XIV: Soles (Sol es) coronare inter corona soles

calibration_role: The capstone topology plate: CUBVS METAMETRICVS gives a genuine 3D substrate (tri-axial reading) and the spherical labyrinth a 2-sphere substrate (great-circle reading). Multilingual logogriphs test the Language-atlas concept directly: the SAME rebus reads in Latin, Spanish, or French depending on chart.
